Package global.thalion.ttio.protection

Class SignatureManager

java.lang.Object
global.thalion.ttio.protection.SignatureManager
public final class SignatureManager extends Object
HMAC-SHA256 signatures with v2 canonical little-endian format for TTI-O datasets.

API status: Stable.

Cross-language equivalents: Objective-C TTIOSignatureManager, Python ttio.signatures.

    Post-quantum signature prefix (ML-DSA-87).

  • Field Details

    • V3_PREFIX

      public static final String V3_PREFIX
      Post-quantum signature prefix (ML-DSA-87).
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Method Details

    • sign

      public static String sign(byte[] data, byte[] key)
      Sign a byte array.
      Parameters:
      data - bytes to sign
      key - 32-byte HMAC key
      Returns:
      signature string in the form "v2:" + base64(hmac)

    • verify

      public static boolean verify(byte[] data, String signature, byte[] key)
      Verify a signature string against data and key. Handles both "v2:"-prefixed signatures and unprefixed v1 signatures.
      Parameters:
      data - original bytes
      signature - signature string to verify
      key - 32-byte HMAC key
      Returns:
      true if the signature is valid

    • sign

      public static String sign(byte[] data, byte[] key, String algorithm)
      Sign data with the named signature algorithm. v0.8 M49.
      • "hmac-sha256" — key is a 32-byte HMAC secret; output is "v2:" + base64(hmac).
      • "ml-dsa-87" — key is the 4896-byte ML-DSA-87 signing key; output is "v3:" + base64(signature).
      Throws:
      CipherSuite.UnsupportedAlgorithmException - for unknown algorithm names.

    • verify

      public static boolean verify(byte[] data, String signature, byte[] key, String algorithm)
      Verify signature against data. Accepts v2: HMAC and v3: ML-DSA-87. The algorithm parameter must match the stored prefix — mismatches raise CipherSuite.UnsupportedAlgorithmException so callers do not silently pass verification of a file signed with a different primitive.
      Parameters:
      data - original bytes
      signature - stored signature string (with prefix)
      key - HMAC key (32 bytes) or ML-DSA-87 public key (2592 bytes), depending on algorithm.

    • hmac

      public static byte[] hmac(byte[] data, byte[] key)
      Compute raw HMAC-SHA256 bytes.
      Parameters:
      data - bytes to authenticate
      key - 32-byte HMAC key
      Returns:
      32-byte HMAC digest

    • canonicalBytes

      public static byte[] canonicalBytes(double[] data)
      Canonicalise a double[] to little-endian bytes for signing.
      Parameters:
      data - signal samples
      Returns:
      little-endian byte representation

    • canonicalBytes

      public static byte[] canonicalBytes(int[] data)
      Canonicalise an int[] to little-endian bytes for signing.
      Parameters:
      data - integer samples
      Returns:
      little-endian byte representation

    • canonicalBytes

      public static byte[] canonicalBytes(long[] data)
      Canonicalise a long[] to little-endian bytes for signing.
      Parameters:
      data - long samples
      Returns:
      little-endian byte representation

    • canonicalStringBytes

      public static byte[] canonicalStringBytes(String s)
      Canonicalise a variable-length string for compound signing. Format: uint32_le(len) || utf8_bytes. A null string produces a 4-byte zero-length prefix with no payload.
      Parameters:
      s - string to canonicalise (may be null)
      Returns:
      canonical byte representation

    • testKey

      public static byte[] testKey()
      Canonical signature test key: key[i] = (byte)((0x5A ^ (i * 7)) & 0xFF) for i in [0,32).
      Returns:
      32-byte deterministic test key

    • signGenomicRun

      public static Map<String,String> signGenomicRun(StorageGroup runGroup, byte[] key, String algorithm)
      M90.2: sign every signal channel and every genomic_index column under one /study/genomic_runs/<name>/ group in one call, storing each signature on the dataset's @ttio_signature attribute.

      Returns a map from "<sub>/<dataset>" (e.g. "signal_channels/sequences", "genomic_index/positions") to the prefixed signature string. Datasets that don't exist on disk are silently skipped (e.g. encrypted files have segments instead of plaintext signal channels).

      algorithm dispatches identically to sign(byte[], byte[], String) — "hmac-sha256" (default) or "ml-dsa-87" (PQC).

      Cross-language equivalents: Python ttio.signatures.sign_genomic_run, Objective-C TTIOSignatureManager#signGenomicRun:. M90.2

    • signGenomicRun

      public static Map<String,String> signGenomicRun(StorageGroup runGroup, byte[] key)
      signGenomicRun convenience overload defaulting to "hmac-sha256".

    • verifyGenomicRun

      public static boolean verifyGenomicRun(StorageGroup runGroup, byte[] key, String algorithm)
      M90.2: verify every signal channel and every genomic_index column under one genomic run. Returns true iff every present, signed dataset verifies under key.

      A dataset that was signed but is now tampered returns false. A dataset that exists but has no @ttio_signature attribute also returns false — that's intentional, since a partial-signature run is not a fully-signed run. Datasets that don't exist on disk are skipped. M90.2

    • verifyGenomicRun

      public static boolean verifyGenomicRun(StorageGroup runGroup, byte[] key)
      verifyGenomicRun convenience overload defaulting to "hmac-sha256".
